title: Japan trounces Tunisia 4-0 and moves closer to World Cup round of 16
summary: With two goals from Ueda and a strong collective performance, the Japanese team achieved its biggest win in World Cup history.

title: U.S. and Iran begin direct talks in Switzerland with mediators
summary: Delegations began negotiations in Geneva to address the situation in Lebanon and other issues, while Trump hints at new sanctions.

title: Fire at Tokyo elementary school causes damage but no casualties
summary: The fire started in a music room and spread quickly; teachers had difficulty using rescue equipment.

title: 5-year-old boy disappears after leaving a hot spring bath in Kagoshima
summary: The child apparently left through a window and is feared to have fallen into a river; authorities continue the search.

title: Toshikazu Toita wins Ishioka mayoral election after previous mayor's removal
summary: The former provincial deputy narrowly defeated the mayor who had been removed by two no-confidence votes.

title: Polls show division in Japan over imperial succession reform
summary: Public opinion is divided over the adoption of members from the former imperial house, while support for the government grows.

title: Spain soundly defeats Saudi Arabia in World Cup
summary: The Spanish team won convincingly in its Group G match, adding three key points.

title: Speculation grows about resignation of UK Prime Minister Starmer
summary: Media report that Starmer could announce his resignation on June 22, though his office denies it and Trump claims he will resign.

title: Actress Mari Hoshino to be the runner for '24-Hour TV' charity marathon
summary: The initiative aims to raise funds for social causes, and this year the main runner will be the well-known actress.

title: Sweden loses 5-0 to Netherlands and prepares to face Japan
summary: The Swedish team suffered a tough defeat that forces them to react ahead of the crucial match against Japan for a spot in the round of 16.

title: Bolivia declares state of emergency over protests and roadblocks
summary: The Bolivian government activated the measure due to mobilizations demanding political changes that are affecting supplies.

title: Zelenskyy claims Ukrainian drone attack on Russian refinery at 2000 km
summary: The Ukrainian president said his forces hit an oil facility in Russian territory with an upgraded drone.

title: Volcanic alert raised for Mount Aso due to eruption risk
summary: The Japan Meteorological Agency raised the alert level to 2 due to the possibility of major eruptive activity.

title: King Charles and Prince William to publish their tax returns
summary: The British royal family seeks to increase transparency by making public the heirs' tax payments.

title: China's rare earth magnet exports to Japan fall 34.6% in May
summary: The reduction in shipments reflects trade tensions between the two countries, though the overall decline is smaller.

title: Analysts warn about impact of Strait of Hormuz crisis on Japan
summary: Experts point out that Japan's dependence on Gulf oil makes it vulnerable to any escalation in the region.

title: Osaka rugby school declares 'serious situation' over alleged bullying
summary: The educational institution activated the protocol after detecting bullying behaviors among students on the rugby team.

title: Chinese fentanyl organization operates in Japan with cryptocurrency fraud
summary: Authorities are investigating a network that uses the country as a base to launder money through digital asset scams.

title: Sociologist Sumio Segayama passes away at 85
summary: The renowned Japanese academic, a specialist in the sociology of education, left a significant legacy in his field.

title: Ninja certification exam attracts 131 candidates from Japan and abroad
summary: The exam, which evaluates traditional ninja skills, sparks global interest as part of cultural heritage.
